description.task: Write a Java program that uses at least 5 methods from the java.lang.Math class and prints a formatted result for each.
instructions: Use Math.abs(-15) and print 'Absolute value of -15: 15'.
Use Math.sqrt(144) and print 'Square root of 144: 12.0'.
Use Math.max(34, 67) and print 'Max of 34 and 67: 67'.
description.hint: java.lang is the default package and is always imported. Math.pow returns double; Math.round returns long.
description.expectedOutput:
Absolute value of -15: 15
Power 2^8: 256.0
Square root of 144: 12.0
Max of 34 and 67: 67
Round 9.7: 10

description.task: Write a Java program that imports java.util.Scanner to read a user's name and age from the console, then prints a greeting.
description.context: The java.util package must be explicitly imported. Scanner lets you read user input from System.in.
instructions: Add the import statement: import java.util.Scanner;
Use sc.next() or sc.nextLine() to read the name.
Use sc.nextInt() to read the age.
Print the greeting in the exact format: 'Hello, Alice! You are 22 years old.'
description.hint: If using nextLine() after nextInt(), you may need an extra sc.nextLine() call to consume the leftover newline. Test with input: Alice and 22.
description.expectedOutput: Hello, Alice! You are 22 years old.

description.task: Demonstrate the concept of packages by creating a class inside a named package and using it from a main class with an import statement.
description.context: Packages organise classes into namespaces and prevent naming conflicts. The package declaration must be the first statement in a Java source file.
instructions: Run the starter code and observe the output.
In a comment block, explain: what a package declaration looks like, what the import statement format is, and why packages are useful.
Submit the code with the explanation comment.
description.hint: A package declaration is: package com.myapp.utils; placed before any class definition. The import statement is: import com.myapp.utils.ClassName;
description.expectedOutput:
Hello, Learner! Welcome to Java packages.
Hello, Java Dev! Welcome to Java packages.
